date.raw_text: During the next several weeks / Over the next two months

events.summary: Over the next two months, 20 potential bidders, including Party A, CSC/Pamplona, Party B and Party C, entered into confidentiality agreements with Mac-Gray.
Mac-Gray engaged BofA Merrill Lynch in connection with Mac-Gray's consideration of a possible acquisition of CSC during the 2011-2012 discussions.
notes: Engagement related to possible acquisition of CSC by Mac-Gray, not the later company sale process.

events.summary: The Board authorized management to work with BofA Merrill Lynch to explore transactional opportunities available to Mac-Gray, including with Party A.
Party A indicated it might consider a business combination with Mac-Gray during a call with BofA Merrill Lynch.

The Board discussed whether to approach third parties regarding a potential sale transaction and the risks and benefits of commencing a process for confidential information review and indications of interest.
The Board formed the Transaction Committee to assist in the administration of the strategic alternatives review, including a sale process.
The Transaction Committee determined to terminate BofA Merrill Lynch's prior engagement letter from the 2011-2012 CSC discussions because that sale process had ended.
notes: Termination of earlier advisory engagement, not termination of the current Mac-Gray sale process.

events.summary: The Board unanimously approved the engagement of BofA Merrill Lynch as Mac-Gray's financial advisor for the exploration of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process.
Mac-Gray entered into an engagement letter with BofA Merrill Lynch for the Board's review of strategic alternatives, including a potential sale process.

At an orientation meeting, participants discussed that if Moab were interested in an equity rollover in a sale transaction arising from the strategic alternatives process, Mr. Rothenberg would need to be excluded from the process.
Party A submitted an unsolicited proposal to acquire Mac-Gray for $17.00 to $19.00 per share in cash.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $17.00 to $19.00 per share

events.summary: The Board established the Special Committee of independent directors to explore strategic alternatives, evaluate proposals for a possible sale or other significant business transaction, and recommend any transaction to the Board.
The Special Committee determined to approach 50 parties to solicit potential interest in a transaction with Mac-Gray.
notes: This is target-run outreach to potential acquirors, captured as process initiation within the supplied taxonomy.

events.summary: Pamplona expressed interest in a transaction, including an acquisition of Mac-Gray by CSC.
Party A expressed that there would be considerable synergies in acquiring Mac-Gray and that it would speak with financing sources regarding such a transaction.
Party B entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray.
Party C entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray.
CSC/Pamplona entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray.
Mac-Gray instructed NDA-signing parties to submit preliminary indications of interest by July 23, 2013.
CSC/Pamplona submitted a preliminary indication of interest at $18.50 per share in cash.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $18.50 per share

events.summary: Party B submitted a preliminary indication of interest at $17.00 to $18.00 per share in cash.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $17.00 to $18.00 per share

events.summary: Party C presented an oral preliminary indication of interest at $15.00 to $17.00 per share in cash.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $15.00 to $17.00 per share

events.summary: Party C submitted a written indication of interest at $16.00 to $16.50 per share in cash.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $16.50 per share

events.summary: Party A entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Mac-Gray.

BofA Merrill Lynch, as instructed by the Special Committee, sent letters to Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona instructing them to submit revised written proposals by September 9, 2013 if they wished to proceed further.
Party B submitted a revised indication of interest at $18.50 per share in cash.
notes: Party B proposed financing with equity from affiliated funds and third party debt, and the revised indication did not include a firm financing commitment.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $18.50 per share

events.summary: CSC/Pamplona submitted a revised indication of interest at $19.50 per share in cash and requested two weeks of exclusive negotiations.
notes: Pamplona was stated to be committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $19.50 per share

events.summary: Party C indicated in a telephone call that it was submitting a revised oral indication of interest at $16.00 to $17.00 per share in cash.
notes: The revised indication did not include a firm financing commitment.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $16.00 to $17.00 per share

events.summary: Party A submitted a revised indication of interest at $18.00 to $19.00 per share in cash.
notes: The revised indication did not include a firm financing commitment.

terms.raw_text: all-cash purchase price of $18.00 to $19.00 per share

events.summary: The Special Committee instructed BofA Merrill Lynch to request final indications of interest from Party A, Party B, Party C and CSC/Pamplona by September 18, 2013.
Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest in the September 18 round.
events.drop_reason_text: Party C did not submit a revised indication of interest or reiterate its prior indication of interest and did not specify any reasons.

events.summary: Party B submitted a revised indication of interest valuing the transaction at $21.50 per share, consisting of $19.00 cash at closing plus options valued by Party B at $2.50 per share.
notes: Party B stated the options represented 10% of the new equity with a strike price equal to Party B's initial cost basis.
Party B stated it valued the options at $2.50 of incremental value per share.

terms.raw_text: valued at $21.50 per share, including $19.00 of cash to be paid at closing and the remaining per share price to be paid in the form of options

events.summary: Party A reiterated that its prior $18.00 to $19.00 per share all-cash indication of interest was its best and final offer.
notes: The filing describes this as a reiteration in a telephone call of Party A's previous indication of interest.

events.summary: CSC/Pamplona submitted a proposal to acquire Mac-Gray for $20.75 per share in cash and requested two weeks of exclusive negotiations.
notes: Pamplona reiterated it was committed to provide 100% of the capital required to consummate the transaction.
Proposal requested exclusive negotiations for two weeks.

events.summary: CSC/Pamplona agreed to increase its all-cash purchase price to $21.25 per share as its last and best offer, subject to a two-week period of exclusive negotiations.
notes: Mac-Gray had proposed moving forward on an exclusive basis if CSC/Pamplona increased price to $21.25 per share.
CSC/Pamplona also requested a two week period of exclusive negotiations.

events.summary: Mac-Gray entered into the merger agreement to be acquired for $21.25 per share in cash.
notes: The chronology blocks in this chunk stop before the dated execution sentence, so execution date is supported by cross-filing evidence.
The merger agreement provided for acquisition by a wholly-owned subsidiary of CSC ServiceWorks, Inc.

events.summary: Mac-Gray executed the merger agreement on October 14, 2013.
notes: Cross-filing evidence supplies the per-share merger consideration.

date.raw_text: October 15, 2013, before the U. S. stock markets opened

events.summary: Mac-Gray and CSC jointly announced the execution of the merger agreement before U.S. stock markets opened on October 15, 2013.

exclusions.explanation: General ongoing review of business plans and strategic alternatives described as continuing activities without a distinct dated sale-process event.
References to discussions between Mac-Gray and CSC about Mac-Gray acquiring CSC are outside whole-company bids for Mac-Gray and not target sale-process events.
Discussion of process for hiring a financial advisor and management roles did not itself constitute a taxonomy event.
May 15, 2013 letter to BofA Merrill Lynch implemented the previously recorded May 14 determination to terminate the old engagement and is not recorded separately.
Board meeting items and appointment of Transaction Committee chairman were governance matters, not sale-process taxonomy events.
Financial advisor candidate presentations are part of advisor selection process but are not standalone taxonomy events.
Proxy contest and election of Moab nominees are background governance facts, not a sale-process event under the taxonomy.
Management presentation of projections and discussion of strategic alternatives structure and timing did not by itself constitute a distinct taxonomy event.
Moab rollover discussions and denial of exclusivity for rollover discussions are process governance matters, not taxonomy sale-process events.
Party A confidentiality agreement negotiations before August 5 did not result in an executed agreement during that time period.
Management presentations and due diligence sessions are not event types in the permitted taxonomy.
The sentence about parties being furnished an informational package and instructed to submit by July 23, 2013 appears twice in the same block.
Reference to the Party A June 21 proposal is a recap of an earlier event not newly described in this chunk.
Briefings to Mr. MacDonald and his views on rollover support or price are not taxonomy events.
Board, committee, diligence, rollover, and merger-agreement negotiation updates without a taxonomy event to record in this call.
Exclusivity agreement proposals, negotiations, execution, and voting-agreement negotiations are outside the permitted event taxonomy.
Special Committee meeting discussing final terms, fairness opinion, and recommendation to the Board is board-process context, not a taxonomy event.
Board approval and recommendation of the merger agreement is governance context, not a taxonomy event.
Pamplona commitment letter delivery and voting agreements becoming effective are not taxonomy events for this extraction call.

unresolved_mentions: CSC and Pamplona are referred to together as CSC/Pamplona for bidding purposes, but the chronology also mentions each separately; kept both the joint bidder and the separate named entities due to potential ambiguity in actor granularity.
The filing states three nationally recognized investment banks were invited to submit proposals, but only BofA Merrill Lynch is named; the other two financial advisor candidates are unidentified.
extraction_notes: Extraction limited to supplied chronology blocks through B048 plus cross-filing appendix where relevant; later process events are not included in this chunk.
